quote: plants creeping in through the window where m and d usually wash dishes... plants stealing coins in the middle of the night... then they go back to their pots, and pretend like nothing happened - see the sickening smile on their little face.
image: Potted houseplants creeping over the kitchen sink at night, clutching stolen gold coins in leaf-claws, then sitting innocently back in their pots wearing tiny sickening smiles.
note: The most iconic invented-world image in the whole journal; a full little narrative in one tableau.

quote: Mine was a monkey whose eyeballs dropped out from a string when you twisted the ears on the side.
image: A foam monkey mask held up in a college critique, its eyeballs dangling out on strings the moment its ears are twisted.
note: Self-contained, absurd, and instantly picturable.

quote: Also dreamt that J went to walk his golden dog - made out of solid gold.
image: A man walking a heavy, gleaming dog cast entirely from solid gold on a leash down an ordinary sidewalk.
note: One clean dream image, deadpan and gorgeous.

quote: there were these sorts of half people who were probs ai cause they didn't have quite enough feelings, and their eyes were replaced by hard boiled eggs, and I swiped each of them.
image: A long bright hallway of expressionless half-people whose eyes are peeled hard-boiled eggs, as a hand swipes past each face.
note: Strong uncanny dream tableau.

quote: I went into his basketball hoodie, and I was the ball, and he was the basket. And he made up a poem... 'I am the basketball, you are the basket - when things get better, it will be fantastic.'
image: A girl curled entirely inside a boy's oversized basketball hoodie, only her face peeking out, held like a ball in a basket.
note: Sweet, physical, and self-explaining.

quote: Wesley, Michelle, and I, who felt like the 4 jews wandering in the dessert, looking for a place to rest our tired heart - found three massage chairs.
image: Three friends slumped in a row of coin-operated mall massage chairs at a party while the rest of the group stands around watching them get vibrated.
note: A perfect deadpan everyday tableau.

quote: BIRDS WITH COMPASSES ON THEIR BACK (BECAUSE THEY NEED TO SEE WHICH DIRECTION THEY'RE GOING)... They kept making shapes with each other, and I thought they were trying to say things to me.
image: Small black velvet-and-wire birds flying in formation across a sky, each with a little blue compass strapped to its back, spelling shapes in the air.
note: Her invented craft-creatures at their most enchanted.

quote: when I finally got out, the sun was only an outline on the horizon - a full outline, but the meat had left... some were dancing and doing things, and maybe wearing dinosaur costumes and I danced with them.
image: A dusk beach where the sun is just a hollow outline on the horizon, its 'meat' gone, and figures in dinosaur costumes dance on the sand.
note: A recurring missed-sunset motif crystallized in one dreamlike scene.

quote: I have a little piece of green glass in one of the black socks I'm wearing right now - long, so as not to show off, I can feel it when I walk. It reminds me of summer ;)
image: A single sliver of green bottle-glass tucked inside a black sock on a walking foot, glinting against the dark wool.
note: A tiny, private, sensory object with a whole mood in it.

quote: he fed me a sugar cube like a horse and I put some of it in my pocket cause it was too much sugar and I hadn't eaten all day.
image: A boy holding a white sugar cube out on a flat palm to a girl the way you'd feed a horse, in a spare room among sketched-out furniture.
note: Quietly aching and very specific.

quote: bruise blocks - ten blocks, and each is different/various ugly discolored bruises. None are the same. These don't fit together in any particular way.
image: A neat wooden crate of ten toy building blocks, each hand-painted as a different mottled green-and-purple bruise.
note: Her craft-object logic at its darkest and most memorable.

quote: he was having the most beautiful panic attack last night and I didn't do anything - head in his hands, shaking, emitting a low-pitched noise. All I could see was the top of his head - his blonde hair.
image: A boy folded over with his head buried in both hands, only his blonde crown visible, a girl beside him watching and not reaching out.
note: Charged, still, and unresolved.

quote: We sat by the edge of a cliff - a cliff made out of sand, and the edge was toppling over - if we wanted to, if we let it... he held me tight and told me he'd never felt this way about anyone.
image: Two people sitting at the crumbling lip of a sand cliff above the waves, the edge sifting away beneath them as he holds her tight.
note: Romantic and precarious in one frame.

quote: (pre-used wishes) yes, these eyelashes have already been wished on, but they are still pretty good :) (same with these dandelions).
image: A little shop display of 'pre-used wishes': fallen eyelashes and gone-to-seed dandelions arranged on a tray with a hand-lettered tag saying they've already been wished on but are still pretty good.
note: A tiny invented product with a real pang under the joke.

quote: he had a little plastic drawer, just for peanut butter cups. Within easy accessibility, and it was almost empty, there were 2 left …2, he gave/left me.
image: The interior of a lived-in car, a small plastic organizer drawer pulled open to reveal two lone peanut butter cups left as a gift.
note: A whole tender relationship inside one little drawer.

quote: She was mad at her roommates for coming in her room and drew a picture of her jumping on them - haha! - it was amazing.
image: A mother reading an old El Salvador diary aloud, holding up a childlike sketch of her younger self gleefully jumping on top of her roommates.
note: A drawing-within-a-drawing; warm and mischievous.

quote: I will buy myself 100 valentines…little heart boxes - Satin, that I can allow velvet to drape into... print angels on little pillows... And then I will have 100 valentines for myself…and I will keep them.
image: A girl alone amid a hundred little satin heart-boxes and velvet-draped pillows printed with angels, all valentines she made for herself.
note: Defiant, crafty, and quietly sad.

quote: there was a toaster-looking heater who was programmed with AI and it was very nice to me, but it was also talking about thinking it may have been programmed wrong, because it shouldn't get this hot.
image: A small toaster-shaped space heater glowing red on a nightstand, fretting aloud that it may have been programmed wrong because it shouldn't be getting this hot.
note: An anxious little appliance, dream-logic and endearing.

quote: we saw a dying sea lion - frothing at the mouth, waving it's head back and forth.
image: A sea lion beached on the wet sand of the PCH, frothing at the mouth and swinging its head slowly side to side.
note: A stark, real, wrenching roadside image.

quote: I got a small glass of milk... then said 'oh but…can I have just a little bit of milk'... so I got to dip my cookie in for a sip.
image: A tiny glass of milk on a bakery counter, a cookie dipped into it for one careful sip, negotiated triumphantly.
note: A small, funny, victorious ritual she retold to everyone.

quote: There was a wedding. you placed tulips, I pulled Begonias from the ground... I wore pink. You also wore pink. No one told us we couldn't both wear pink, and so we did.
image: Two people in matching pink at their own imagined wedding, one laying tulips while the other yanks begonias up by the roots, both laughing.
note: The most sustained, luminous fantasy passage in the book.

quote: just: a train of friends. each train car can be hooked to the next one... Each person is a Big Head. And that is nice. To go around together like that.
image: A little toy train chugging in a circle where each car is a giant friend's Big Head, all hooked together and rolling along.
note: A lonely wish rendered as a sweet, silly contraption.
